standup.name: Smit Parekh
inProgress: Exited Employees register — push the app branch and open the PR (backend already merged to dev)
Relieving letter template — commit the change and verify a sample PDF
next: Run Exited Employees locally against the 226 stage records to eyeball the toolbar + infinite scroll
Generate a sample relieving-letter PDF to confirm the template formatting matches the .docx

tasks.name: Exited Employees — Read-Only Register (Backend + Frontend)
tasks.body: New Admin/HR screen listing every employee who has left (pulled live from users.exited_at) with search, department and exit-date filters, Excel export, and infinite scroll
tasks.summary:
- Found 226 of 545 stage users have an exit date but only 3 have a COMPLETED employee_exits record — the rest are legacy, so the Offboarding screen never surfaced them
- Backend: UserRepository.findExitedUsers (exited_at IS NOT NULL + department/date/search filters, ORDER BY exit date DESC, explicit countQuery), ExitedEmployeeService/Impl, ExitedEmployeeController (/api/exited-employees + /export Apache POI xlsx), Admin/HR only
- Frontend: ExitedEmployeesScreen with a consistent toolbar (search + department dropdown + Exit from/to date pickers), infinite scroll like the Employees table, sidebar nav gated to Admin/HR (hrAdminOnly)
- No migration — read-only over existing data; backend merged to dev, app committed (not yet pushed)

tasks.name: Leave — Block Paid Leave During Notice Period
tasks.body: Employees with an active resignation can no longer apply Casual (paid) leave — only LWP; the paid-leave option is hidden in the apply UI
tasks.summary:
- Backend: added ResignationService.hasActiveResignation and a guard in LeaveRequestServiceImpl.createLeave that rejects CASUAL while an active resignation exists
- Frontend: hide the Casual leave option and show a lock notice in the apply modal while resigned; LWP still allowed
- Applies to existing resignations automatically — it checks the current exit state, not the submit time
- Merged to dev (backend PR #193/#194, app PR #213)

tasks.name: Relieving Letter — Reproduce Monarch Template
tasks.body: Rewrote the relieving-letter generator to match the official Monarch template wording/format, filling the employee placeholders with a gender-aware salutation
tasks.summary:
- Read the Relieving Letter TEMPLATE .docx and mapped placeholders EMP_NAME / EMP_ID / DESIGNATION / LAST_DATE / RESIGN_DATE
- Rewrote RelievingLetterServiceImpl.buildHtml to reproduce the template verbatim — fixed signatory (Pritesh Patel, Managing Director) and full footer (CIN / GSTIN / DUNS / phones / email)
- Gender-aware Mr./Ms. salutation via the existing PronounSet; top Date = resignation date per the template
- Kept the settlement-finalized gate and the generate/release/download flow unchanged; backend compiles (uncommitted)

tasks.name: Merge dev — Resolve AppSidebar Conflict
tasks.body: Pulled dev into feat/exited-employees and resolved the AppSidebar.tsx merge conflict
tasks.summary:
- Kept dev's new PMS nav section (Vendors / Projects / Item Catalogue / Purchase Requests + pmsKey gating) alongside my Exited Employees item
- NavItem interface carries both pmsKey and hrAdminOnly; filterItem keeps both gates
- Completed the merge commit; backend had no conflict and is already in sync with dev
